description: Lets ONLY the logged-in twinme.me web app bridge its auth tokens into the OS keyring (store_auth_token + store_refresh_token) and mint a fresh access token from the stored refresh token (get_fresh_access_token), so the headless clip/meeting sync can authenticate and keep itself + the webview signed in without the refresh cookie WebView2 drops. Still narrow: one remote origin, one window, only these three auth-bridge commands, and NO core/plugin APIs are exposed to that remote origin.
//windows: Scope to the MAIN window only. The remote TwinMe app loads there (www/index.html redirects to https://twinme.me). The 'hummingbird' overlay is intentionally excluded — it never sets the token.
//remote: remote.urls (Tauri 2 CapabilityRemote, URLPattern syntax) is what extends this capability to a REMOTE origin. Without it, twinme.me (loaded remotely in the main window) gets NO access to store_auth_token. We allow only the apex and its subdomains over https; no http, no wildcards on other domains. 'local' defaults to true so the bundled www/ onboarding page is also covered, which is harmless (it doesn't call this command).
//permissions: ONLY the autogenerated app-command permissions for the three auth-bridge commands. Tauri's build script auto-generates 'allow-<command>' / 'deny-<command>' for every command in generate_handler! (and build.rs commands()); app-command permissions are referenced with NO namespace prefix. We expose exactly these three and nothing else — no core:*, no plugin perms — so the remote origin cannot reach window/shell/fs/notification/etc. APIs through this capability.
